title: Describe the capabilities and function of TFTP/FTP in the network

questions.question: Which command restore configuration to the running-config for a device from a server?

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Switch#copy tftp: running-config".

incorrect.explanation: **Switch#archive tftp: running-config** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch#archive tftp: running-config" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Switch#restore tftp://192.168.1.2 running-config** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch#restore tftp://192.168.1.2 runnin…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Switch#copy server: running-config** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch#copy server: running-config" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Eliminate answers that describe a different protocol, port, or command than the stem asks for.

questions.question: Which command begin the upgrade of an IOS from a TFTP server?

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Switch#copy tftp flash".

incorrect.explanation: **Switch#copy tftp ios** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch#copy tftp ios" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Switch#copy tftp nvram** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch#copy tftp nvram" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Switch#upgrade tftp flash** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Switch#upgrade tftp flash" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Eliminate answers that describe a different protocol, port, or command than the stem asks for.

questions.question: Which command can boot a router from a TFTP server for the image of c2900-universalk9-mz.SPA.151-4.M4.bin on the TFTP server of 192.168.1.2?

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Router(config)#boot system c2900-universalk9-mz.SPA.151-4.M4.bin tftp://192.168.1.2".

incorrect.explanation: **Router#boot tftp://192.168.1.2** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router#boot tftp://192.168.1.2" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Router(config)#boot tftp://192.168.1.2 c2900-universalk9-mz.SPA.151-4.M4.bin** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router(config)#boot tftp://192.168.1.2 c…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Router(config)#boot system tftp://192.168.1.2 c2900-universalk9-mz.SPA.151-4.M4.bin** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router(config)#boot system tftp://192.16…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Eliminate answers that describe a different protocol, port, or command than the stem asks for.

questions.question: You’re upgrading the flash memory on a 2900 router with a brand-new flash card. What needs to be done to restore the IOS?
choices: The new flash memory will have a mini-IOS installed. You will need to upgrade it from the mini-IOS.
The router will boot into the ROMMON, and from there you will need to TFTP download the IOS.
Nothing needs to be done because the IOS is not storage on the flash memory card.
Format the flash card with the FAT operating system and copy the IOS image to the card.

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "The router will boot into the ROMMON, and from there you will need to TFTP download the IOS.".

incorrect.explanation: **The new flash memory will have a mini-IOS installed. You will need to upgrade it from the mini-IOS.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "The new flash memory will have a mini-IO…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Nothing needs to be done because the IOS is not storage on the flash memory card.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Nothing needs to be done because the IOS…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Format the flash card with the FAT operating system and copy the IOS image to the card.** does not answer the requirement in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Format the flash card with the FAT opera…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Eliminate answers that describe a different protocol, port, or command than the stem asks for.

questions.question: Which command(s) are required before you can use an FTP server for backing up configuration? Assume that the username is USER and the password is USERPASS.
choices: Router(config)#ip ftp username USER password USERPASS
Router(config)#ip ftp username USER Router(config)#ip ftp password USERPASS

correct.explanation: The correct answer is "Router(config)#ip ftp username USER Router(config)#ip ftp password USERPASS".

incorrect.explanation: **Router(config)#ip ftp username USER password USERPASS** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router(config)#ip ftp username USER pass…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Router(config)#ftp USER password USERPASS** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router(config)#ftp USER password USERPAS…" without matching the scenario constraint

incorrect.explanation: **Router(config)#username USER password USERPASS** does not answer which in this stem. .
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Selecting "Router(config)#username USER password US…" without matching the scenario constraint

answerReview.examTip: Eliminate answers that describe a different protocol, port, or command than the stem asks for.
